Name: A Kid Named Cudi
Notes:
(10/07/04) (Scott Mescudi moves to New York, Kid Mesc becomes KiD CuDi)
(07/17/08) ('A Kid Named Cudi' officially releases)
(2004-2008)
Type: "A Kid Named Cudi" is the debut mixtape by Kid Cudi, released in 2008 by independent record label "Fool's Gold Records" & New York City streetwear brand 10 Deep. The album was Cudi's first big success, establishing his fan base, being reviewed well by critics, and catching the ear of hip-hop superstar Kanye West, who would sign Cudi to G.O.O.D. Music later that year. Three of the mixtape's songs, "Man On The Moon," "Day N Nite," and "TGIF," would later make it onto Cudi's debut album "Man on the Moon: The End of Day." In June 2008, Cudi quit his job at the Bape store.

Name:
Man On The Moon: The Guardians
(The Demo Tape)
Notes:
(07/17/08) ('A Kid Named Cudi' officially releases, and Cudi keeps working on music at Avex Recording Studio)
(07/23/2009) (Demo tapes start leaking online and Cudi decides to redo a lot of the album.)
(2008-2009)
Type: Kanye & Kid Cudi would work on "808s & Heartbreak" at this time, and go on to grow in popularity, leading to the release of "Man On The Moon: The Guardians." This was the original version and concept for "Man On The Moon: The End Of Day." At this time Cudi was giving out CD demo tapes to people around New York. Around July 23rd, 2009, Kid Cudi had his demo tape start leaking online. We are uncertain if it was a result of an email being hacked or a CD having been left in a car. Cudi decided to redo most of the album and scrap a lot of material. The final product was the released "Man On The Moon: The End Of Day."

Name:

(Man On The Moon)
Notes:
(07/23/2009) (Demo tapes start leaking online and Kid Cudi decides to redo most of the album)
(9/15/2009) ('Man On The Moon: The End Of Day' officially releases)
(2009)
Type: "Man on the Moon: The End of Day" is the debut studio album by Kid Cudi. It was released on September 15, 2009, through Dream On, GOOD Music, and Universal Motown Records. Narrated by fellow American rapper Common, it follows the release of his first full-length project 'A Kid Named Cudi' (2008), and is the first installment of the Man on the Moon trilogy. Production was handled by several high-profile record producers, including Kanye West, Emile Haynie, Plain Pat, and Jeff Bhasker, as well as contributions from Dot da Genius, Free School and The Kickdrums, among others.

(11/9/2010) (Man On The Moon II: The Legend Of Mr. Rager officially releases)
(2010)
Type: "Man on the Moon II: The Legend of Mr. Rager" is the second studio album by American rapper Kid Cudi. It was released on November 9th, 2010, through Dream On and G.O.O.D. Music, and distributed by Universal Motown Records. It serves as a sequel to his debut studio album "Man on the Moon: The End of Day," and is the second installment of the Man on the Moon trilogy. The album incorporates alternative and psychedelic elements to the sound that Cudi explored in his previous album. It features a blend of dark and emotional lyrics, exploring themes of depression, loneliness, detachment, and isolation. The album also highlights other topics, such as Cudi's former cocaine addiction, fame, and alcoholism, as well as family issues and women. Evolved from 'Cudder' ('Cudder: The Revolution Of Evolution')

Name: A Man Named Scott
Notes:
(01/26/2011) (Kid Cudi announces the mixtape on Twitter)
(08/21/2011) (Kid Cudi tweets the mixtape is scrapped)
(2011)
Type: In January of 2011, Cudi would announce a mixtape on Twitter that was to serve as a sequel to 'A Kid Named Cudi' named 'A Man Named Scott.' The mixtape was created as a sort of companion piece to 'WZRD'. the rock album collaboration album with producer Dot Da Genius, as Cudi expressed that Wizard wouldn't be rap focused at all, and that fans of his rapping should stay tuned for 'A Man Named Scott' instead. In August of 2011, Cudi would again take to Twitter to announce that the mixtape had been cancelled in order to focus on 'WZRD' and 'Man On The Moon III'.

Type: "WZRD" is the debut studio album by American alternative rock duo WZRD, composed of musicians Dot da Genius and Kid Cudi. It was released on February 28, 2012, via Universal Republic Records and GOOD Music, as well as Cudi's newly found label imprint, Wicked Awesome Records. The album was supported by the singles "Brake" and "Teleport 2 Me, Jamie." For the project Cudi went sober from everything and used it as inspiration for the album.

Name: INDICUD
Notes:
(06/05/2012) (Kid Cudi announces 'INDICUD' on Twitter, dedicated to Ben Breedlove)
(04/16/2013) ('INDICUD' officially releases)
(2012-2013)
Type: "INDICUD" is the third studio album by American rapper and singer Kid Cudi, released on April 12, 2013. The album marked a departure from Cudi's earlier style, as he took on a more prominent role in production and instrumentation. The album features a mix of introspective, personal lyrics, and a darker, more experimental sound, and includes guest appearances from artists such as Kendrick Lamar, A$AP Rocky, and Michael Bolton. 'Indicud' was well-received by fans and critics alike, with many praising Cudi's growth as an artist and the album's innovative sound. The lead single from the album, "Just What I Am," was a commercial success and helped to solidify Cudi's position as a unique and influential voice in hip-hop and alternative music.

Name: Satellite Flight: The Journey To Mother Moon
Notes:
(10/16/2013) (Kid Cudi announces he will be releasing an EP as a prelude to his next project)
(02/25/2014) ('Satellite Flight: The Journey To Mother Moon' officially releases)
(2013-2014)
Type: At a concert in Dallas is the first mention of the project, then announced on the 27th of October, 2013, by DatNewCudi on Twitter. 'Satellite Flight: The Journey to Mother Moon' is the fourth studio album by American rapper and singer Kid Cudi. It was released on February 25, 2014, and was originally intended to be an EP, but was later expanded into a full-length album. The album features a more experimental sound, with a greater emphasis on live instrumentation and a more cohesive, concept-driven narrative. The album received generally mixed reviews, with many praising its introspective, atmospheric sound and Cudi's continued evolution as an artist. Despite its critical acclaim, 'Satellite Flight: The Journey to Mother Moon' performed modestly commercially, but remains a fan favorite and a highlight in Kid Cudi's discography.

Name: Passion, Pain & Demon Slayin
Notes:
(12/05/15) (Kid Cudi releases Speedin' Bullet 2 Heaven)
(12/16/2016) (Passion, Pain & Demon Slayin officially releases)
(2015-2016)
Type: On 05/11/2016, Kid Cudi addressed "Man On The Moon III" rumors and revealed 'PP&DS.' On October 5th, 2016, Cudi would check into rehab for depression and suicidal urges. The album features a mix of introspective and introverted lyrics, which showcase Kid Cudi's personal struggles and journey towards self-discovery. The album draws inspiration from various genres including alternative, hip-hop, and rock, and features guest appearances from Travis Scott, Pharrell Williams, and Willow Smith. The album received positive reviews from fans and critics, who praised its raw and emotional themes, as well as Kid Cudi's unique and authentic approach to music. 'Passion, Pain, & Demon Slayin'' is considered one of Kid Cudi's best works and is a must-listen for fans of alternative hip-hop.

(06/08/2018) ('Kids See Ghosts' officially releases)
(2016-2018)
Type: Out of the five Wyoming projects from 2018, people consider "KIDS SEE GHOSTS" to be one of the best. This album focuses heavily on overcoming struggles caused by mental health, which Kanye and Cudi had been fighting. It is characterized by many psychedelic and rock-influenced elements, making for an album that sounds like nothing else. This album was officially released on June 8th, 2018, making it Kanye's second collab album.

(2018-2019)
Type: Not a lot is known around this time; however, on July 22nd, 2019, Cudi announced 'Entergalactic' along with the show, expecting a 2020 release. This would get delayed for various reasons, but Cudi would go on to state he finished 'Entergalactic' in 2019/2020. We do not know how much this changed from the 2022 release. It may be worth noting near the end of this era Travis Scott and Kid Cudi were working on 'THE SCOTTS'.

Name: Man On The Moon III: The Chosen
Notes:
(??/??/2019) (Kid Cudi makes "2 or 3" songs that don't fit the current projects, he turns them into 'Man On The Moon III: The Chosen') 
(12/11/2020) ('Man On The Moon III: The Chosen' officially releases)
(2019-2020)
Type: "Man on the Moon III: The Chosen" is the (alleged) final installment in Kid Cudi's highly acclaimed 'Man on the Moon' trilogy. The album showcases the rapper and singer's versatility and range as an artist, blending elements of hip-hop, R&B, and alternative rock to create an emotional and thought-provoking musical journey. The album features introspective lyrics that explore themes of self-discovery, growth, and resilience. This would be worked on at the same time as 'THE SCOTTS' album, which would later be confirmed canceled in late 2022, then in April 2023 Cudi stated "maybe" to the idea of the album again.

Notes:
(6/23/2019) (Netflix announces that Kid Cudi would write and produce an animated TV series based off of Cudi's album 'Entergalactic')
(9/30/2022) ('Entergalactic' is officially released along with its TV series counterpart)
(2022)
Type: "Entergalactic" is the eighth studio album by American rapper and singer Kid Cudi, released on September 30, 2022. The album is the soundtrack to the Netflix animated series of the same name, which was created and written by Kid Cudi himself. 'Entergalactic' features a mix of hip-hop, psychedelic rock, and electronic music, and showcases Cudi's versatility as an artist. The album's concept revolves around a fantasy version of Scott's life and is centered around themes of love, adventure, and sorrow. Meant to feel like "an adventure around a city and a new chapter of life."

(1/12/2024) (Cudi releases INSANO)
(2022-2024)
Type: In late 2022, Kid Cudi announced his retirement, with one last album to finish it off. Eventually in February 2023, Cudi stated he was no longer retiring and there was more to come. It is speculated to build on multiple 2021 tracks. The first single was released June 2nd, 2023, while the full album will release September 22nd, 2023 and is a collaborative effort with DJ Drama. Named after a scene from the movie "Waterboy."

Name: FREE
Notes:
(2/23/2024) ("INSANO (NITRO MEGA)" is released)
(5/9/2025) (Kid Cudi releases "Neverland" in May)
(8/22/2025) (Kid Cudi releases "FREE")
Type: After the release of "INSANO (NITRO MEGA)," Kid Cudi announced on Twitter that his next album was already underway. By August 2024, he confirmed the album was already complete, and he would rather focus on his comic book and acting career for the time being until he drops it. Cudi would then release the single "Neverland" on May 9th, 2025, also announcing the album's title "FREE," stating that it would be Pop-Oriented. Cudi then released FREE on August 22nd, 2025. It's worth mentioning that King Chip teased "The Almighty GloryUs" in October 2024, potentially meaning the scrapped collaboration album between the two could release at some point. Kid Cudi also teased a potential fourth entry in the Man On The Moon series in an anniversary post for Man On The Moon II.
